review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: Tata Motors is a standout in the automotive industry for its innovation, commitment to quality, and strong emphasis on sustainability. As one of India's largest and most respected automobile manufacturers, Tata Motors has made significant strides in electric vehicle technology, contributing to a greener future. Their vehicles are known for being robust, reliable, and affordable, catering to a wide range of customers. The company also has a global presence, with products that are well-regarded in international markets. Additionally, Tata Motors is part of the Tata Group, a conglomerate renowned for its ethical business practices and social responsibility, which adds to the brand's trustworthiness and appeal.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2: While Tata Motors has many strengths, there are some challenges it faces as well. For instance, the company has struggled with consistency in the quality and refinement of its vehicles, especially in the past. Some models have faced criticism for issues related to fit and finish, as well as the overall driving experience compared to competitors in certain segments. Additionally, despite making strides in electric vehicles, Tata Motors still has a long way to go to catch up with global leaders in terms of technology and infrastructure. The company also faces stiff competition in both domestic and international markets, which can make maintaining market share challenging.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9:
1. Employee policies

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
Joined after my MBA but made me work like I was holding an ITI certificate. Really not sure where to start and where to end this topic. 
1. Extremely toxic work culture, no corporate dignity. 
2. Being publicly abused was a everyday story
3. Absolutely zero work/life balance. If (work/life) was a fraction, the number would tend to infinity on a graph sheet
4. Be ready to work on all weekends. Alternate Saturday time off (on paper) is actually a myth in TML
5. No comp off's. Don't ask for one (else you'll circle back to Sl.No2)
6. Pathetic office infrastructure. Plastic chairs, chipped desks provided, that too on sharing basis
7. After completing evening shift they want you to report next morning at 0600hrs. Forced me couple of times to undergo the ordeal
8. Efficiency is a direct measurement of the hours you overstay
9. Please don't go by the company name, that's all that remains. 10.Policies and culture once the company once known for is no more

I could go on and on...but fortunately now I have better things to do in life

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9:
People often say that the first job you have is the one that will determine your next course of life. If you want to make movies, join a filmmaking company. If you have the next best idea for an app, apply as a junior application engineer to some of the cutting-edge start-ups.

But when you’re looking for your first job, what comes to the top of your priority list? Putting aside the basic requirements like salary, paid time off (PTO), and the nature of the job itself, what would working in your dream company look like?

For some, it’s joining the ranks of the top industry leaders (think Google, Amazon, Microsoft, Apple) and rubbing shoulders with the most brilliant minds; for others it could be starting small and finding your footing in a small- or medium-sized enterprise (SME).

An SME is a business with less than 500 employees, whereas a big company is categorised by its size and ability to dominate in their industry. Think of it as a David vs Goliath situation (size-wise), where SMEs are David, and big companies are Goliath!

Career opportunities are plentiful across all industries, from entry-level roles right up to managerial. Truthfully, you’ll find that the job descriptions are the same across all industries and businesses, no matter how big or small the company is.

Regardless of how many employees a company has or its annual profits, a writer is going to write, a designer is going to design, and an IT analyst is going to be the default go-to person for any IT-related issue (like helping people figure out why they can’t connect to the WiFi)!

So if the job scope and salary range are all the same, would jumping into a big or small company be more beneficial for your career? Ultimately, it all boils down to your preferred management style, benefits, career growth, and work-life balance. Let's take a look at the pros and cons for both to help you make a more well-informed decision.

Big Businesses, Big Benefits
The advantages:

1) A good benefits package/resources

Learning Opportunities: Interns at Tata Motors have the chance to gain hands-on experience and insights into various aspects of the automotive sector, from engineering to marketing. Networking: You'll have the opportunity to build a strong professional network within the company and potentially in the broader automotive industry. Skill Development: Internships at Tata Motors often involve challenging projects that can help you develop critical skills, both technical and soft skills, which are highly valued in the job market. am a PGET of batch 2017.

When I was to join Tata Motors (TML), even I used to search for the answers to this question on quora.

To begin with, on your joining, the first 4 days will be an induction session where senior officials will interact with you and there will be plenty of game activities.

Then, for the next 20 days or so, all joinies will be taken through the departments, the sub-departments, and their roles and functions. Thereafter, you will be assigned your department and you will start learning slowly and with time, you will be given responsibilities. It will take around 3–4 months to understand your work and start delivering.

Working days: Alternate Saturday off and all Sundays

Working hours: 8.30 to 5

18 leaves for the first 1 year and 48 leaves every year afterwar

At Tata Motors, Pune ERC, NVH CAE and NVH Test departments in the company offer ample work responsibilities and hence various learning opportunities.

Financial growth is little sluggish in the company at least for last 4-5 years.
Only first and third Saturdays are OFF for the company.
You may be asked to come in shifts even after 4-5 years of experience.

review-details_detailsLink__NxIv5: Why to chose or not chose Tata Motors as a career option.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9:
Learning: 
1) Learn by imitating superiors.
2) Numerous of meetings to put forth your Ideas.
3) If wiling can work on trans-departmental assignments. 
Pay:
1) Decent as per standard of living of the city.
Career growth:
1) Opportunities like FTSS and TAS. 
Work life balance:
1) With a little of plus and minus, one would get plenty of family time.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
Learning:
1) No directed skill building approach. This results in very slow growth in one's market value.
2) Meeting is agenda-less and it is unlikely that people be interested in your Ideas.
3) People are highly result driven. This leads to a very short sighted skill building which further lead to slow career growth.
4) Process of organisation are very primitive. This result in non-suitability with other key players in market. To put in other words, if one is looking for job change let's say after 5 years , top MNCs may not value one's work-ex.    
Pay: 
1) Below the industry average.
Career growth:
1) Though they offer opportunities like FTSS and TAS, it is unlikely one would bag the fruits without an MBA. One may also get a feeling that Diploma and B.tech doesn't carry much of significance to year on year basis  growth without an MBA.
Bottom line:
1) If you like to banter around all day, easy life and slow career  growth and secure one go for Tata Motors but if you are young, have a spark of ambition look for some better opportunity.

Everything is a con in Tata Motors Ltd.
Non-core functions like HR, GDC, and IT might be a good place to work but core functions like SCM, Operations, Production planning are pathetic.

Even during Covid they are forcing employees to come back in office. Everyone gets shouted on. Calls start from 6:30 in the morning and people start saying if you would have been in office instead of being at home you would know how work is done. Work from home is a facade here. If u take WFH or some leave because of health reasons, they will start calling you saying you have laptop you do that, even though you are on Sick leave. People who have lost family memebers during Covid are being called back by their bosses and they say everyone is dying why should work stop becasue of that. You question your bosses' idea once and they decrease your rating. There have been cases of some persons who give chocolate to their bosses and get good rating. (This is a true story). Once you are on your notice period things get more bad. You will be engaged in ridiculous projects which have no relation to you at all or are stuck because of some absurd reasons. Bosses' start claiming you have to work till last day, I know how to get work done from guys like you. 
Do not go in this company. 
Your personal life will get destroyed too.
